description: Lightweight Google Drive sync with path-based index, conflict detection, and Smart/Push/Pull modes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

name: NeoGDSync

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
Fix Obsidian Community Portal manifest parsing by using string-valued fundingUrl entries instead of an array-valued custom entry.

Assets included for Obsidian release scanning:
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css

Validation:
- manifest.json, package.json, and versions.json parse as JSON
- manifest version is 1.0.6 and versions.json maps 1.0.6 to minAppVersion 1.6.0
- release assets are present at repo root and non-empty
releases.name: v1.0.6 — fix manifest fundingUrl schema

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description: OAuth proxy switched to neogdsync-worker (Web Application client). Plugin auto-migrates legacy proxy URLs on load.
releases.name: v1.0.5 — switch to Web Application OAuth client

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Bug fix

Existing installs with the old `authProxyUrl` (`ogd.richardxiong.com`) were being redirected to the wrong OAuth page.

**Fix:** on plugin load, the legacy URL is automatically migrated to the self-hosted Cloudflare Worker (`neogdsync-oauth.neogdsync.workers.dev`). No manual action needed — update and reconnect.
releases.name: v1.0.4 — fix auth URL migration

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## What's new

### One-tap Google Drive setup
Settings now shows a **Connect Google Drive** button. Tap it, log in with your Google account in the browser, and copy the refresh token from the page — no manual OAuth steps required.

### How to connect
1. Open Obsidian → NeoGDSync settings
2. Tap **Connect Google Drive**
3. Approve access in the browser
4. Copy the token shown on the page
5. Paste it into the **Refresh token** field

### Changes
- Worker: added `GET /authorize` and `GET /callback` endpoints
- Plugin settings: connect/disconnect UI based on auth state
- Default `authProxyUrl` updated to `neogdsync-oauth.neogdsync.workers.dev`
releases.name: v1.0.3 — Google Drive OAuth connect flow

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
Fix portal review: remove word "Obsidian" from manifest description

- manifest.json: description no longer contains "Obsidian" (portal requirement)
- Add funding URLs: Ko-fi + NOWPayments (stablecoin)
- Self-hosted OAuth proxy (CF Worker) from v1.0.1
releases.name: v1.0.2

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
Replace third-party OAuth proxy with self-hosted Cloudflare Worker

- Remove dependency on ogd.richardxiong.com (third-party, closed-source proxy)
- Add self-hosted Cloudflare Worker proxy (oauth-proxy/) — zero deps, auditable, HTTPS
- Secrets stored in Cloudflare Secrets, never in code or git
- No functional change for end users
releases.name: v1.0.1

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## v1.0.0

First stable release.

### Bug fixes since v0.2.2
- **Fix folder renames stuck in pendingOps forever** — renaming/moving a folder in Obsidian registered it as a `'create'` op that could never be pushed (folders have no file content). This caused the plugin to permanently show `☁ N pending` even after force-push. Fix: folder renames skip `pendingOps`; folders are created on-demand when needed. Defense-in-depth: any pre-existing stuck folder entry self-clears on the next sync.

### Install via BRAT
Add `martinlegend/neogdsync` in BRAT → "Add Beta Plugin".
releases.name: v1.0.0 — Stable release

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

Files inside a newly-created subfolder (e.g. `2026/Attachments/Nala Project/nala.pdf`) were silently skipped during smart sync on other devices.

**Root cause:** The `unknownChanges` loop in `smartSync()` skipped new Drive folders without registering them into `folderIdToPath`, so when a file's parent folder was also new, the parent lookup failed and the file was silently dropped.

**Fix:** 2-pass loop — Pass 1 registers new folders into `folderIdToPath`, Pass 2 resolves files using the now-complete folder map.
releases.name: v0.2.2 — Fix new-subfolder files not syncing to other devices

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## v0.2.1

- **Cross-folder rename data loss:** Moving a file to a different folder now correctly relocates it on Google Drive (addParents/removeParents). Previously file stayed in old Drive folder and was lost when that folder was deleted.
- **Silent delete failures:** handleDelete no longer swallows Drive API errors. Failures surfaced in sync error summary; index entry preserved for retry.
- **Drive trash propagation** (v0.2.0): Smart sync treats file.trashed===true as deletion.
- **CSS review fix** (v0.2.0): Replaced inline element.style.* with CSS class.
releases.name: 0.2.1 — Fix cross-folder rename data loss + surface delete errors

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Per-folder force push/pull

The Sync modal now has an optional **Folder** field. If filled, Force Push and Force Pull operate only on that vault-relative folder subtree (e.g. `2026/2026-04`).

- Force Push with folder: runs offline diff first, filters pendingOps to prefix
- Force Pull with folder: partial Drive index rebuild for that subtree only
- Graceful error if folder not found on Drive
- Prefix matching is exact (no false matches)

Also: fix manifest description case for community plugin review bot.

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Fixes
- **`deleteFile` now uses Drive trash instead of permanent delete.** Previously the plugin called HTTP `DELETE`, which bypassed Drive trash and made any accidental local deletion (or buggy delete propagation) unrecoverable. It now PATCHes `trashed=true`, giving users the standard ~30-day recovery window.
- **`smartSync` no longer resurrects trashed files.** When a file was trashed on Drive, the changes batch typically also contained an earlier `modified` event with `removed=false`. If the local pathIndex no longer had that fileId, the modified event walked the `unknownChanges` path, fetched metadata, and re-pulled the file locally — silently undoing the trash. The fix skips changes whose `file.trashed` is set, and double-checks `meta.trashed` after the live metadata fetch as defence-in-depth.

## Migration notes
Files deleted by smartSync from this version onward will land in Drive trash and can be restored from there for ~30 days. No data-format changes; safe to upgrade in place.
releases.name: 0.1.19 — Trash-handling bug fixes

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## What's fixed

### Bug: specific files permanently stuck, never pulled on receiving device

If a device had a stale `pendingOps` entry for a file, and the main sync loop's `handlePush`/`handleConflict` returned early without adding the path to `result.pushed/pulled`, the file was permanently blocked:

- The stale pendingOp was never cleared
- `pullNewFromDrive` skipped the path every sync cycle
- Drive's newer version was never downloaded

This manifested as a specific file (e.g. `WuDao/Power of 1% everyday.md`) never updating on iPad even after repeated Mac pushes, while other files in the same folder synced fine.

**Fix**: Replace `if (this.pendingOps[path]) continue` with a `handled` Set built from `result.pushed + result.pulled + result.deleted` after the main loop. Only successfully processed paths are skipped; stale pendingOps fall through to the normal pull.
releases.name: 0.1.18 — Fix stale pendingOps permanently blocking cross-device pulls

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

### Fix: iPad / multi-device files missing after sync (Bug 1)
`smartSync()` used to silently discard Drive change events whose `fileId` was not in the local index. This caused files created from another device (iPad, web, etc.) to never appear locally when the index was stale.

**Fix:** For unknown `fileId`s, call `getFileMeta()` to resolve the file name and parent folder, reconstruct the local path, pre-populate the index entry, and let `pullNewFromDrive` download the file normally. If the parent folder is also missing from the index, a `warn` is logged instead of silently dropping.

### Fix: Conflict resolution overwrote newer Drive content (Bug 2)
`handleConflict()` was pushing the local offline version to Drive (overwriting the newer remote content) and saving Drive's version as `.conflict.md`. This caused older iPad edits to silently destroy newer Mac edits.

**Fix:** Reversed direction — Drive version is now authoritative and pulled to the canonical path. The local offline version is saved as `[base].conflict[ext]` for manual review and merge.
releases.name: 0.1.7 — Fix iPad missing files & conflict resolution direction

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description: Fix: revert ID acronym overcorrection; all eslint-plugin-obsidianmd checks pass

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description: Fix: all remaining Obsidian review bot required issues (v0.1.5)

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description: Fix all Obsidian review bot required issues: requestUrl, typed APIs, console.debug, vault.configDir, sentence case UI, CSS classes, setHeading

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

**Pulled files being re-added to pendingOps after sync**

`writeLocal()` calls `vault.modifyBinary()` which fires Obsidian vault modify events. `handleModify()` sees the file as changed (snapshot not yet updated at event time) and re-adds it to pendingOps. After each sync, the most recently pulled file would appear as a pending op, causing it to be re-synced on next run.

**Fix:** Clear `result.pulled` from pendingOps at the end of smartSync, forcePush, and forcePull.
releases.name: v0.1.3 — Fix pulled files re-added to pendingOps

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description: Fix: pulled files re-added to pendingOps after sync. Fix: PR template, LICENSE, description.

releases.author: martinlegend
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

### Critical: pendingOps flooding fixed

**Root causes fixed:**
1. `mergeOfflineDiff()` ran before `onLayoutReady` — vault stats are stale/zero at that point, flagging all 6000+ files as modified. Moved to inside `onLayoutReady`.
2. `onunload()` save order: `saveSettings()` was before `snapshot.save()`, so fresh snapshot was never persisted. Fixed order.
3. `.obsidian` entries in snapshot: 261 entries that `vault.getFiles()` never returns → all flagged as delete on every restart. Added `.obsidian` to exclude list.
4. Snapshot now stored in `data.json` via `loadData/saveData` (reliable) instead of `.neogdsync/snapshot.json`.
releases.name: v0.1.2 — Fix pendingOps flooding on every restart
